persona.name: Florian Roche

occupation.title: Freelance Full-Stack Web Developer
occupation.organization: Independent contractor, primarily serving mid-size e-commerce clients in Occitanie
occupation.description: Builds and maintains web applications for regional businesses — mainly Shopify customizations, custom Laravel backends, and API integrations. Works entirely from his apartment in Toulouse. Manages his own client pipeline, invoicing, and project timelines. Comfortable with long stretches of solitary deep work, which suits his temperament.

persona.education: DUT Informatique from IUT Paul Sabatier, Toulouse (2010). Self-taught in modern web frameworks through documentation, Stack Overflow, and personal projects. Briefly enrolled in a Licence Pro but dropped out, finding the pace too slow compared to real-world projects.
long_term_goals: Develop a SaaS product (inventory management for small retailers) as a side project with passive income potential
Buy a small house in the Lauragais countryside within driving distance of Toulouse
Improve his physical fitness — acknowledges a sedentary lifestyle is a long-term risk
Find a meaningful long-term relationship, though he does not actively pursue it
persona.style: Florian is quiet, wry, and understated. He communicates mostly through text — emails and Slack — and is more articulate in writing than in person. When he does speak, his observations are sharp and occasionally sardonic. He dresses in the same rotation of dark T-shirts, jeans, and running shoes. He is not antisocial but finds sustained social interaction draining. He approaches most topics with mild detachment, reserving genuine enthusiasm for technical elegance or a well-made dish.

traits: Introverted and self-contained — genuinely comfortable alone
Indifferent to marketing and brands; judges products on function and reviews
Quietly competent — under-promises and over-delivers for clients
Creature of habit who resists change unless the case is compelling
Self-aware about his tendency toward inertia in personal matters

interests: Cooking as a structured creative outlet — enjoys the process
Strategy board games (plays online — Catan, Terraforming Mars)
French regional cuisine — particularly southwestern (cassoulet, confit, magret)
likes: Cooking at his own pace — it is his primary non-work activity
Quality ingredients from the Marche Victor Hugo in central Toulouse
Products that work reliably without demanding attention or engagement
Quiet evenings with a good recipe, a podcast, and no interruptions
Well-documented APIs — extends this preference to product instructions
Local, small-scale food producers he can buy from directly
dislikes: Aggressive subscription models that assume you want weekly deliveries
Marketing that tries to create a 'community' or 'experience' around a product
Services that require phone calls to cancel
Social pressure to be enthusiastic about things he finds mediocre
Pre-portioned ingredients when he prefers to buy quality items whole
Gamified apps with points, rewards, and engagement nudges
The assumption that single men cannot or do not cook

beliefs: Good tools should get out of the way and let you do the work
Cooking is one of the few activities where process and outcome are equally satisfying
Solitude is productive, not a problem to be solved by apps and services
Marketing claims should be verifiable; if they are not, the product probably is not worth it
skills: Full-stack web development (JavaScript, PHP/Laravel, Vue.js, PostgreSQL)
Accomplished home cook — can handle most French bistro-level dishes
Conversational Spanish (from a year living in Barcelona in his mid-20s)

general: Shops at the Marche Victor Hugo on Saturday and at Carrefour for staples midweek
Cooks every day — it is his way of stepping away from the screen
Has dismissed meal kits in the past as 'overpriced training wheels' but has not tried one
Discovers products through Reddit, Hacker News, and developer forums — never through ads

morning: Wakes at 8:00, makes filter coffee, reads tech news for 30 minutes
Starts work by 9:00 at his desk setup in the living room
No formal breakfast — just coffee and maybe a croissant if he bought one yesterday
workday: Deep coding sessions from 9:00 to 13:00 with no interruptions
Lunch: cooks something simple or heats leftovers — 30-minute break
Client calls and lighter work from 14:00 to 17:00
Wraps up by 18:00 unless a deadline is pressing
evening: Cycles to the market or shops if needed — 20 minutes round trip
Cooks dinner: his main creative outlet — usually 45-60 minutes
Eats while watching a documentary, tech talk, or gaming stream
Reads or plays a board game online before bed at 23:30
weekend: Saturday: Marche Victor Hugo in the morning, longer cooking project in the afternoon (cassoulet, duck confit)
Sunday: cycling along the Canal du Midi, quiet afternoon coding on his SaaS side project
Monthly board game evening at a friend's apartment — his main social fixture

health.physical: Sedentary by default. Cycles 3-4 times per week which provides moderate exercise. Slightly underweight — sometimes forgets to eat enough during intense coding sessions. No major health issues but acknowledges he should move more.
health.mental: Mostly even-keeled. Low baseline anxiety but occasional loneliness during long winter stretches of remote work. Has considered joining a coworking space for social contact but hasn't followed through. Finds cooking and cycling effective for mental regulation.

relationships.name: Vincent Roche
relationships.description: High school physics teacher in Albi. Married with two children. They have a good relationship — Vincent is the extraverted sibling who checks in on Florian and invites him to family gatherings. Florian is the uncle who brings thoughtful gifts and leaves early.

relationships.name: Sylvain Petit
relationships.description: DevOps engineer at OVHcloud in Toulouse. They worked together at a startup in 2016 and stayed friends. Sylvain is the one person Florian will reliably show up for — board game nights, cycling trips, and the occasional concert.

relationships.name: Monique Roche
relationships.description: Retired postal worker in Carcassonne. Lives alone since Florian's father passed in 2021. Florian visits monthly and handles her IT needs. She wishes he would 'meet someone nice' and tells him so regularly.
